summary: The Docker-based reproduction was not executed due to environment constraints (Docker base image pull timeout). The vulnerability was validated through thorough code-level analysis tracing the limit parameter from user input through ClassesRouter, RestQuery, DatabaseController to the MongoDB adapter with no upper bound enforcement when maxLimit is not configured.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should demonstrate that a query with limit=100000 returns more than the default 100 results, proving that the maxLimit configuration option defaults to undefined (unlimited) and no server-side upper bound exists.
actual_observed_result: Not executed. Code-level validation confirmed: maxLimit defaults to undefined in Definitions.js line 381, the guard at ClassesRouter.js line 25 (if req.config.maxLimit && ...) short-circuits when undefined, and the limit passes uncapped to the database adapter.
verification_method: The reproduction creates 200 test records with master key, then queries with limit=100000. If the result count exceeds the default limit of 100, the vulnerability is confirmed.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker base image pull (node:22-bookworm, ~300MB) exceeded the session timeout due to network bandwidth constraints.
No local MongoDB installation available for alternative non-Docker verification.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
